chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, CIRCUIT COURT HYDERABAD C.P.No.S-1322 of 2017 DATE ORDER WITH SIGNATURE OF JUDGE(S) 1. For orders on MA-2786/2017 2. For orders on office objections. 3. For orders on MA-2787/2017 4. For katcha peshi. 07.08.2017 Mr. Altaf Hussain Chandio, Advocate for petitioner. = 1. Granted. 2. Deferred for the time being. 3. Granted subject to all just exceptions. 4. It is, inter alia, contended by the learned Counsel for the petitioner that the petitioner contracted marriage with Mst.Fouzia D/o Muhammad Aslam Rajput on her freewill, wish and choice according to Shariat on 12.09.2012 and the said Nikahnama has been registered. In this regard, Mst.Fouzia also sworn her freewill affidavit before the Oath Commissioner, Nawabshah (Shaheed Benazirabad), copies whereof are annexed with the petition as Annexures “A” and “B” at Pages-19 to 22. Per learned Counsel, respondent No.13, the brother of Mst.Fouzia and her father are bent upon to create hindrances in the happy lives of the petitioner and his wife Mst.Fouzia and have also extended threats of dire consequences, in case Mst.Fouzia is not returned to her family. Per learned Counsel, earlier also the petitioner as well as his wife Mst.Fouzia filed C.P.No.S-3343 of 2012 before this Court, Bench at Sukkur and the same was disposed of on 10.12.2012 on the statement filed by the SHO concerned. Per learned Counsel, recently the private respondents No.8 to 16 came to the house of the petitioner at Nawabshah and extended threats and also physically manhandled the petitioner and his wife and asked the petitioner to send Mst.Fouzia back to her parents’ house.

Per learned Counsel, earlier also the petitioner as well as his wife Mst.Fouzia filed C.P.No.S-3343 of 2012 before this Court, Bench at Sukkur and the same was disposed of on 10.12.2012 on the statement filed by the SHO concerned. Per learned Counsel, recently the private respondents No.8 to 16 came to the house of the petitioner at Nawabshah and extended threats and also physically manhandled the petitioner and his wife and asked the petitioner to send Mst.Fouzia back to her parents’ house. Per learned Counsel, keeping in view the malafide intentions of the private respondents, the petitioner has serious apprehension of foul play at the hands of the private respondents, in case the official respondents fail to provide protection to the petitioner. Be that as it may, let notices be issued to the respondents as well as Additional A.G for 25.08.2017. In the meantime, the respondents are directed not to cause any harassment to the petitioner and the official respondents are directed to provide protection to the petitioner in the event the petitioner approaches them, but strictly in accordance with law. JUDGE Shahid
